checks.finding: design.md Architecture, Native module loading contract, Human-copy integrity contract, Risk-policy lexical implementation contract, and ADR Change Log state rationales for build-time generation, fixed script-relative loading, PS5.1 XOR comparison, anchored no-follow copying, deterministic local risk scanning, and the absence of a new ADR.
design.md Open Questions states None and records that the human selected the anchored no-follow design on 2026-07-14; no unresolved implementation question requires an owner or resolution path.

design.md Assumptions asserts that existing constant-parity.tests.sh remains a protected regression suite, but no investigation.md is present in the allowed input manifest and this repository-specific protection-state assertion is neither a reasonable technology default nor explicitly marked as a human-accepted risk. An implementer relying on it could choose an invalid test-placement or protected-copy path. This ungrounded non-trivial assumption is blocking under ASSUMPTIONS-VALID.

requirements.md REQ-001, REQ-003, REQ-004, and REQ-005 impose explicit security and compatibility constraints including cross-runtime write/ambiguity denial, byte-equivalent evidence-path diagnostics and golden fixtures, UTF-8/NUL fail-closed risk-policy behavior, and anchored no-follow publication plus deterministic prefix/rollback recovery. design.md Constraint Compliance contains only five broad rows and provides no compliance statement for these mandatory constraints. Although other design sections discuss parts of them, the check contract requires every requirements constraint to be addressed in Constraint Compliance. The omissions make mandatory fail-closed and exact-output behavior non-auditable and are Critical by the NO-REQ-CONTRADICTION contract.

No response-time, throughput, concurrency, latency, or SLA performance requirement appears in requirements.md. frontend-spec.md Performance Budget additionally bounds the comparator to 32 bytes and prohibits runtime JSON I/O.

design.md Deployment / CI Plan and infra-spec.md identify repository-local Windows and GitHub Actions targets, require generator --check before guard suites, and make stale output or failed tests block the PR. There is no service rollout, incremental feature flag, database schema migration, or new secret; security-spec.md Secrets Management confirms no secret is added.

design.md Data Plan declares static integrity configuration rather than database tables or columns, explicitly states that no stored user-data migration exists, and defines the controlled transition order: add canonical/generated sources, human-copy live declarations, then enforce CI --check. infra-spec.md Rollback defines reviewed restoration and full-batch recovery.

No external or third-party service API call is introduced. GitHub Actions is documented as the CI execution target in design.md Deployment / CI Plan and infra-spec.md, with purpose, workflow contract, and fail-closed behavior. Local Windows native dependencies are named in the Human-copy integrity contract and API / Contract Plan, with unsupported capability failures defined before replacement.

design.md Components, Cross-Layer Dependencies, API / Contract Plan, and Test Strategy cover REQ-001 through REQ-005 without adding a user-facing UI, persistent service, database model, or unrelated endpoint. The extensive human-copy runner and rollback design are directly required by REQ-005 and AC-013.

The security-sensitive guard, HMAC, workflow-escalation, generated-module, filesystem-namespace, hard-link, and partial-install risks each have named TEST-001 through TEST-013 paths in design.md Test Strategy and security-spec.md Security Tests, including static API-contract checks, cross-runtime parity, injected rename failure, digest-state verification, rollback, and CI --check evidence.
SKIP: the allowed input manifest contains no approved domain context map, domain contract, or aggregate card, and this repository-local enforcement feature declares no domain-model surface.
